title: Accessible Puzzle Interface and Multichannel Feedback · Runtime Lens: Multichannel Cues
summary: Walls, visited cells, current focus, route, and errors are communicated only by color and animation, while the Canvas has no usable alternative. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the multichannel cues increment in keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
Provide a synchronized structured-text grid, route or step list, coordinates, and status that convey essential puzzle state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-multichannel-cues — Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-accessible-interface-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_accessible_interface_2(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-multichannel-cues-1qt6dy
# Competency: maze-multichannel-cues.
# Case 2686: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def evidence_maze_multichannel_cues_1qt6dy(essential_states: int = 9, text_equivalents: int = 9, keyboard_actions: int = 9, color_only_states: int = 0, open_barriers: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1qt6dy = "1qt6dy-yd6tq1-2686"
    assert evidence_variant_1qt6dy
    evidence_axes_1qt6dy = (
        "grid1qt6dy",
        "topology2686",
        "searchyd6tq1",
        "event1qt6dy2686",
        "access26861qt6dy",
        "recoveryyd6tq12686",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1qt6dy)) == 6
    if essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0:
        return False, text_equivalents, "accessibility-count"
    if text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states:
        return False, text_equivalents, "equivalent-experience"
    if color_only_states > 0:
        return False, color_only_states, "color-only-cue"
    if open_barriers > 0:
        return False, open_barriers, "unclosed-barrier"
    assert text_equivalents == essential_states
    return True, text_equivalents, "multichannel-maze-interface"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Multichannel Cues structural check is only one gate. keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Multichannel Cues evidence · Theory Multichannel Cues, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Multichannel Cues precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Multichannel Cues evidence · Theory Multichannel Cues, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 6227 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Build Multichannel Cues evidence · Theory Multichannel Cues, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: High-contrast red and green are sufficient distinct cues.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Multichannel Cues model · Theory Multichannel Cues
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Multichannel Cues: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 4608 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile visual maze state with structured text, keyboard control, multichannel cues, and barrier closure; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Multichannel Cues model · Theory Multichannel Cues.
steps.why: Retrieving Multichannel Cues through the concrete keyboard-first visual and structured-text maze interface case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Multichannel Cues model · Theory Multichannel Cues, keep Multichannel Cues connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Communicate wall, focus, visited, frontier, route, success, and error through redundant text, shape, pattern, position, and optional sound.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-multichannel-cues-1qt6dy.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-multichannel-cues-1qt6dy(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_multichannel_cues_1qt6dy\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?essential_states < 1 or text_equivalents < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?text_equivalents != essential_states or keyboard_actions < essential_states)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?color_only_states > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?open_barriers > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
